vulnerabilities.description: The X-Content-Type-Options header is not set. This header can prevent certain types of attacks, such as MIME-sniffing, which can lead to cross-site scripting vulnerabilities.

fix.law: Set the X-Content-Type-Options header to 'nosniff'. This can be done by adding the following HTTP response header: X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff

vulnerabilities.description: The X-Frame-Options header is not set. This header can help protect against clickjacking attacks by preventing the site from being rendered within a frame or iframe.

fix.law: Set the X-Frame-Options header to 'DENY' or 'SAMEORIGIN'. This can be done by adding the following HTTP response header: X-Frame-Options: DENY

vulnerabilities.description: The Content Security Policy (CSP) header is not set. CSP is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, such as cross-site scripting and data injection attacks.

fix.law: Set a Content Security Policy (CSP) header to restrict the sources from which certain types of content can be loaded on your website. This can be done by adding the following HTTP response header: Content-Security-Policy: policy_content

vulnerabilities.flaw: HTTP Strict Transport Security Not Enabled
vulnerabilities.description: HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) is not enabled. HSTS is a security feature that helps to protect websites against man-in-the-middle attacks by forcing browsers to use HTTPS.

fix.law: Enable HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) by adding the following HTTP response header: Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains

vulnerabilities.description: The site is missing Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF) protection. CSRF attacks occur when a malicious website sends a request to a different website on which the victim is authenticated, leading to unauthorized actions.

fix.law: Implement CSRF protection mechanisms, such as adding anti-CSRF tokens to forms and validating those tokens on the server side for each request.

potential_technical_flaws.description: Facebook.com may be vulnerable to XSS attacks if input fields are not properly sanitized and validated, allowing attackers to inject malicious scripts.
potential_technical_flaws.recommendation: Implement proper input validation and output encoding to prevent XSS attacks. Use libraries like Content Security Policy (CSP) and sanitize user-generated content.

potential_technical_flaws.description: Facebook.com may be missing important security headers like Content Security Policy (CSP), Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS), X-Frame-Options, etc., leaving the site vulnerable to various attacks.
potential_technical_flaws.recommendation: Implement security headers like Content Security Policy (CSP), Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS), X-Frame-Options, etc., to enhance the security posture of the website.

potential_technical_flaws.description: Facebook.com may have insecure direct object references that could allow attackers to access unauthorized data or perform unauthorized actions.
potential_technical_flaws.recommendation: Implement proper access controls and validate user permissions on server-side to prevent insecure direct object references.

potential_technical_flaws.description: Facebook.com may be leaking sensitive information through error messages, headers, or verbose responses, which could be exploited by attackers.
potential_technical_flaws.recommendation: Ensure that error messages and responses do not disclose sensitive information to users. Implement custom error handling and disable verbose error messages.

potential_technical_flaws.description: Facebook.com may be vulnerable to CSRF attacks if it does not implement proper CSRF tokens or anti-CSRF mechanisms, allowing attackers to execute unauthorized actions on behalf of users.
potential_technical_flaws.recommendation: Implement CSRF tokens or anti-CSRF mechanisms to protect against CSRF attacks. Validate and verify each incoming request to ensure they are legitimate and initiated by the user.

fix.solution: Implement proper security headers like Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, and X-XSS-Protection to enhance web application security.
Regularly update all software components, libraries, and frameworks to the latest secure versions to mitigate known vulnerabilities.

vulnerabilities.flaw: Missing website encryption (HTTP instead of HTTPS)

fix.solution: Enable HTTPS by obtaining an SSL/TLS certificate to encrypt data transmitted between the server and clients.
Restrict access to sensitive directories, disable directory listing, and ensure that error messages do not reveal sensitive information.

code.example: Disable directory listing in web server configuration

fix.solution: Implement input validation and output encoding to sanitize user inputs and prevent malicious script injections.

code.example: Validate and sanitize user inputs before displaying them
